acceptance: ddx install helix fetches and installs HELIX. ddx search returns results from registry. .ddx/lock.yaml tracks installed packages with commit SHA and tree hash. ddx verify checks integrity.
description: Replace git subtree sync with lightweight package install. ddx install/search/uninstall fetching from configurable registries. Commit SHA pinning + tree hash in .ddx/lock.yaml.
title: Implement library registry with package install (FEAT-009)

acceptance: The authoritative CI path invoked by .github/workflows/ci.yml runs the bead schema validation guard on every CLI validation run; local validation entry points name the same guard or a dedicated equivalent command; intentionally invalid bead-schema fixtures fail deterministically; and the resulting validation commands pass in the repo after wiring.
description: Background: cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json and schema_validation_test.go now define the shared bead envelope guard, but that protection is only effective if the authoritative CI path runs it every time. Wire the schema validation guard into the repo's real validation path in .github/workflows/ci.yml and lefthook so bead-schema drift fails automatically in both local and CI runs. Reuse the existing Go-based validation path unless a narrower explicit schema target is justified; if you add a dedicated target, document the command and make failure output actionable.
title: Enforce bead record schema validation in CI

acceptance: No harness name checks in runner.go. TokenPattern and EffortFormat on Harness struct.
description: runner.go:103 has if harnessName==codex. extractTokens has switch on harness name. Breaks data-driven pattern.
title: Move token patterns and effort format to Harness struct

acceptance: Adapter-level tests verify that DDx propagates the default beads collection and non-default runtime collection names to external backends consistently; tests cover collection normalization and failure behavior where relevant; the tests do not prescribe bd/br file layout or backend-internal storage strategy; and go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
description: Background: ADR-004 now defines named bead-backed collections as the DDx storage abstraction, with JSONL free to map collections to separate files while bd/br own their own physical layout. Harden cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go and related adapter boundaries with tests that verify DDx passes the correct logical collection name for the default tracker collection and for non-default runtime collections used by exec, metric, and agent storage. Keep this at the adapter boundary: do not encode JSONL file naming, multi-file sharding, or any bd/br internal storage assumptions in these tests.
title: Add collection-aware external backend adapter tests for runtime collections

acceptance: Tests verify exact arg arrays for codex, claude, gemini with all flag combos (model, workdir, effort, prompt via arg and stdin).
description: No test verifies the exact args built for codex/claude/gemini given model, workdir, effort, prompt. Core logic untested.
title: Add arg construction tests for all harnesses

acceptance: A consumer guide exists explaining: how to register execution definitions, how to invoke ddx exec run, how to query run history by artifact ID, and what structured result schemas look like. HELIX can follow this guide to integrate without reading DDx internals.
description: HELIX (and other DDx consumers) need a clear integration guide for ddx exec. Currently FEAT-010 specifies the model but there is no consumer-facing documentation explaining how an external tool registers definitions, invokes runs, and queries history.
title: Document consumer integration contract for ddx exec

acceptance: The governing DDx artifacts explicitly define: (1) the role of MET artifacts in the graph, (2) how executable metric definitions link to artifacts, (3) how observation-history records link to metrics, and (4) deterministic behaviors for validation, execution, comparison, append, inspect, and trend queries. Follow-on implementation tasks can reference these artifacts without requiring DDx to understand HELIX-specific workflow stages.
description: Background: FEAT-005 defines MET-* as a generic artifact convention in the DDx document graph, but it does not yet describe how executable metric definitions or stored metric observations should relate to those artifacts. Evolve the governing DDx artifacts so the boundary is explicit: a MET artifact declares the metric and its place in the authority graph, while executable measurement definitions and historical observations are separate DDx-native records linked back to artifacts by ID and relationship. Resolve where executable metric configuration lives, how history records identify the governing metric artifact, and which DDx commands or APIs own validation, execution, comparison, and history inspection. Generate deterministic acceptance criteria and concrete tests for those behaviors before implementation work begins.
title: Clarify MET artifact boundaries and define metric runtime/history links

acceptance: Execution definitions with executor.kind=agent run successfully through ddx agent; each exec run retains a deterministic link to the underlying agent session ID and captured evidence; ddx exec history, ddx exec result, and ddx exec log expose the delegated run coherently without inventing a separate runtime store; failure and timeout behavior are test-covered; and go test ./internal/exec ./internal/agent ./cmd -run TestExec passes.
description: Background: FEAT-010 and TD-010 define agent as a first-class executor kind for ddx exec, and FEAT-006 / TD-006 define the canonical agent session capture model. The current implementation still returns a not-yet-implemented error when an execution definition uses executor.kind=agent. Implement delegation from ddx exec run to ddx agent so one execution run retains a deterministic link to the underlying agent session identity, captured prompt/response evidence, and structured run result. Preserve the generic execution record model: ddx exec owns the execution definition and run row, while ddx agent remains the canonical prompt/response session logger. Add deterministic command and store tests for delegation, correlation metadata, failure handling, and history inspection.
title: Implement ddx exec agent executor delegation via ddx agent

acceptance: The DDx tracker contains dependency-gated child tasks that: (1) make the MET artifact boundary explicit at the artifact-graph level, (2) define DDx-native executable metric and observation-history primitives linked to artifacts by ID and relationship, and (3) implement runtime execution plus durable history storage with deterministic tests. DDx remains artifact-generic and does not hardcode HELIX workflow stages or document progressions.
description: Background: DDx knows about artifacts and the relationships between them, but today MET-* only exists as an artifact convention in the document graph. That is enough to express that a project cares about a metric, such as startup time, without forcing DDx to understand HELIX workflow stages or document taxonomies. What is still missing is a DDx-native runtime boundary for executable metric definitions and a durable storage mechanism for metric observations over time. Those runtime/history layers should remain workflow-agnostic and link back to artifacts by ID and relationship, rather than teaching DDx about FEAT->SD->TD->TP progression. Scope this tranche to clarifying that boundary, defining the runtime/history model, and implementing the reusable DDx primitives needed to run, record, inspect, and compare metric results over time. Require every child task to evolve the governing DDx artifacts and acceptance tests as necessary before implementation lands.
title: Add executable metric runtime and historical tracking to DDx

acceptance: FEAT-006 and any necessary downstream design/test artifacts define full agent session capture and inspection behavior unambiguously; acceptance criteria cover persistence format, CLI inspection behavior, config-driven log paths, compatibility with existing logs, and failure modes; and the spec names concrete tests to add under cli/cmd and cli/internal/agent before implementation begins.
description: Background: FEAT-006 already says ddx agent session logs should let operators inspect the prompt and response, but the current implementation only persists metadata such as prompt length, token count, duration, and exit code. Evolve the DDx specs before implementation: update FEAT-006 and any impacted architecture/design docs, and create missing SD/TD/TP artifacts as needed, to define full session-body capture, inspection UX, retention or redaction policy, backward compatibility for existing session entries, and any correlation metadata needed by workflow tools. Generate deterministic acceptance criteria and explicit tests as part of this design work. If the current feature text is too loose, tighten it before code changes begin.
title: Evolve FEAT-006 for full agent session capture and inspectable logs

acceptance: Constant removed. Build passes.
description: types.go:81 defines DefaultAutomation=auto but nothing references it.
title: Remove dead DefaultAutomation constant

acceptance: `bead.id_prefix` is a valid field in `.ddx/config.yaml`; `ddx bead create` uses the configured prefix instead of repo-name autodetection when the field is set; server and CLI bead paths resolve the same configured prefix; existing env-var override behavior remains defined and tested; and deterministic tests cover config validation plus ID generation with configured and default prefixes.
description: Background: FEAT-004 documents bead ID prefix override via `bead.id_prefix` in `.ddx/config.yaml`, but the current implementation only reads `DDX_BEAD_PREFIX` or falls back to repository-name autodetection. That means projects like Niflheim cannot pin a stable short prefix such as `nif` through repo-local DDx config, and adding the field today would fail schema validation. Validate whether any existing config path already supports this; if not, wire `bead.id_prefix` end-to-end through config schema/types, CLI/server bead store construction, and any relevant documentation so repo-local config is authoritative and consistent.
title: Add repo-local bead ID prefix configuration support

acceptance: ddx metric resolves through ddx exec; there is no separate authoritative .ddx/metrics runtime store; metric history, comparison, and trend views derive from exec runs; and any migration or backward-compatibility behavior is documented and test-covered.
description: Convert the metric convenience surface to a projection over the generic execution substrate. Preserve any explicit compatibility or migration policy, remove the authoritative .ddx/metrics store, and keep metric history/trend behavior derived from exec runs.
title: Re-layer metrics over ddx exec

acceptance: ddx doc migrate converts all dun: frontmatter to ddx: preserving all fields. Round-trip test passes.
description: One-time tool: ddx doc migrate converts dun: frontmatter blocks to ddx: in all markdown files in a directory tree.
title: Migration tool: convert dun: frontmatter to ddx:

acceptance: Run() calls resolveOpts(), buildArgs(), execute(), processResult(). Each method <30 lines.
description: Run() does 8 things: resolve, validate, build args, execute, time, error handle, extract tokens, log. Too many responsibilities.
title: Decompose runner.Run() into smaller methods

acceptance: ddx agent run persists full inspectable session content together with existing metadata; ddx agent log shows recent sessions and ddx agent log <id> returns the full session detail promised by FEAT-006; configured session_log_dir is honored; existing session entries remain readable or migrate cleanly; and tests cover capture, inspection, compatibility, and error behavior.
description: Background: DDx agent is intended to replace HELIX's custom harness logging, but the current log only records session metadata and not enough detail to inspect what prompt was sent or what response came back. Implement the FEAT-006 design for full session capture in DDx, persisting prompt and response bodies or equivalent attached payloads alongside structured metadata, keeping config-driven log directory support, and preserving reasonable behavior for failed or large runs. If the design task creates a narrower governing artifact, update this issue's spec-id before coding. Generate deterministic command-level and internal tests instead of relying on manual runs.
title: Implement full ddx agent session capture and inspectable logs

acceptance: Runner.Run() uses injected Executor. No direct exec.CommandContext calls in runner.go. Mock executor used in tests.
description: F-001: runner.Run() directly calls exec.CommandContext. Extract Executor interface (Execute ctx,binary,args,stdin -> stdout,stderr,exitCode,error). Inject real in prod, mock in tests.
title: Extract Executor interface from runner.Run() for testability

acceptance: ddx exec definitions and runs are stored under .ddx/exec; validate/run/log/result/history are implemented and test-covered; writes are atomic or serialized under concurrent writers; and the implementation exposes a shared substrate that projections can reuse without separate storage.
description: Build the artifact-generic execution substrate described by FEAT-010 and architecture. Store definitions and immutable runs under .ddx/exec, support validate/run/log/result/history, and make writes atomic under concurrent use.
title: Implement generic ddx exec substrate

acceptance: ddx doc stale detects stale docs. ddx doc stamp updates review hashes. ddx doc graph shows dependency tree. Reads dun: frontmatter for backward compat. All tests pass.
description: Port doc_dag, frontmatter, hash, stamp from dun to ddx. Read both ddx: and dun: frontmatter, write ddx:. CLI: ddx doc graph/stale/stamp/show/deps/dependents/validate.
title: Implement document dependency graph (FEAT-007)

acceptance: ddx agent run uses default harness and model overrides from .ddx/config.yaml when no CLI flags are provided.
description: agentRunner() in agent_cmd.go ignores .ddx/config.yaml. Should load agent.harness, agent.models, agent.timeout_ms, agent.session_log_dir from project config.
title: Wire agent config loading from .ddx/config.yaml

acceptance: The DDx tracker contains execution-ready child tasks for bead ergonomics and agent session capture; each child names the governing DDx specs to evolve, requires deterministic acceptance criteria and tests, and keeps standalone metric/research tooling out of scope for this tranche.
description: Background: generic work item storage is already in DDx and agent dispatch is intended to be in DDx, but HELIX still carries wrapper-only behavior because DDx is missing a few workflow-agnostic primitives. Scope this tranche to bead ergonomics and agent session capture only: explicit claim ownership, append-only execution evidence on beads, and full inspectable agent session bodies. Leave standalone ddx metric and ddx research surfaces out of scope for now. Every child task must evolve the DDx specs where necessary before coding, then generate deterministic acceptance criteria and tests rather than relying on implicit behavior.
title: Close DDx gaps for HELIX experiment support without adding metric/research tools

acceptance: agent log reads session_log_dir from runner config, not hardcoded default.
description: agent_cmd.go:248 hardcodes agent.DefaultLogDir. Ignores project config session_log_dir.
title: Fix agent log command to use runner config for session_log_dir

acceptance: The review produces one durable alignment report, one review issue for the execution-model drift, and any follow-up execution issues needed to reconcile the plan/code mismatch.
description: Top-down alignment review for the FEAT-010 execution substrate. Review the current metric-specific runtime/store implementation against the generic ddx exec planning stack and generate deterministic follow-up issues only if the review finds real gaps.
title: HELIX alignment review: FEAT-010 execution model

acceptance: FEAT-004 and SD-004 are updated to define the intended claim and evidence primitives; any missing downstream TD/TP artifacts are created or explicitly deferred with rationale; the resulting acceptance criteria cover CLI syntax, storage shape, compatibility, and read/query behavior deterministically; and the spec names exact tests and fixtures to add under cli/cmd and cli/internal/bead before implementation begins.
description: Background: FEAT-004 and SD-004 cover bead storage, concurrency, and queue derivation, but HELIX still wraps ddx bead because the CLI hardcodes claim ownership and there is no first-class append-only execution evidence trail for close summaries or experiment outcomes. Evolve the DDx specs before implementation: update FEAT-004 and SD-004, and create any missing TD/TP artifacts needed, to define explicit claim assignee control, generic execution evidence comments/events, storage and migration rules, read/query semantics, and any API or MCP exposure that follows from current DDx contracts. Generate deterministic acceptance criteria and concrete test cases as part of the spec work. If the current specs are ambiguous, fix the specs instead of coding against assumptions.
title: Evolve FEAT-004 / SD-004 for assignee-aware claims and execution evidence

acceptance: The documented DDx metric runtime surface is implemented for validation, execution, and comparison; executable metric definitions resolve their linked metric artifact identity; result normalization and comparison follow the governing DDx artifacts; and deterministic tests cover invalid definitions, execution failure modes, result parsing/aggregation, and compare semantics.
description: After the metric artifact boundary is clarified, implement the workflow-agnostic DDx runtime for executable metrics. Add the machine-readable definition format and command surface needed to validate metric definitions, run them reproducibly, normalize captured samples/results, and compare runs using the semantics defined in the governing DDx artifacts. Keep the model artifact-generic: metric definitions and runs should link back to metric artifacts by ID rather than encoding HELIX workflow stages or repo-specific autoresearch files. Add the deterministic unit and integration coverage called for by the updated acceptance artifacts.
title: Implement executable metric validation, execution, and comparison

acceptance: docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md exists with ddx: frontmatter, describes system structure, component interactions, data flow, and deployment topology.
description: No architecture doc describes how CLI, server, doc graph engine, bead storage, and agent dispatch compose. Feature specs describe individual capabilities but nothing ties them together.
title: Create architecture document

acceptance: TestIntegration_Codex and TestIntegration_Claude send trivial prompts and verify non-empty output. Skipped when binary not on PATH.
description: No test calls a real harness. Arg construction bugs ship undetected.
title: Add integration tests against real harnesses

acceptance: Web UI served at / from ddx-server. Document browser lists and renders docs. Bead board shows status/deps. Dependency graph renders visually. Agent log shows sessions.
description: Vite + React + Tailwind SPA embedded in ddx-server via Go embed.FS. Document browser, dependency graph visualization, bead board, agent log monitor.
title: Build web UI with embedded SPA (FEAT-008)

acceptance: ddx-server starts on localhost, serves /api/documents, /api/beads, /api/docs/graph, /api/docs/stale. MCP tools ddx_list_documents, ddx_read_document, ddx_list_beads, ddx_bead_ready work.
description: Go web server (Chi or net/http) serving documents, beads, doc graph, and agent session logs over HTTP REST and MCP (Streamable HTTP via mcp-go). Single binary.
title: Implement DDx server with HTTP API and MCP endpoints (FEAT-002)

acceptance: DDx exposes a deterministic way to append and inspect bead execution evidence/comments/events; stored entries survive round-trip and preserve ordering; existing beads remain readable; and tests cover create/append/read behavior, JSONL compatibility, and malformed-record handling where relevant.
description: Background: HELIX experiment and close flows need a generic place to record execution evidence, but DDx beads currently only have mutable notes plus unknown-field preservation. Implement the FEAT-004 / SD-004 design for an append-only evidence trail in DDx, using a generic bead comment or event primitive rather than HELIX-specific ad hoc fields. Keep JSONL compatibility, preserve ordering deterministically, and make the resulting behavior inspectable from the DDx CLI. If the design task narrows the governing artifact set, update this issue before implementation. Generate deterministic tests for creation, append, read, and compatibility paths.
title: Implement append-only bead execution evidence for workflow history

acceptance: DDx CLI supports claim and unclaim flows with explicit assignee control without wrapper-side field injection; claim metadata remains deterministic and backward-compatible; help text and docs match the shipped CLI surface; and tests cover CLI parsing, store mutation, compatibility with existing beads, and failure behavior.
description: Background: generic work-item storage already lives in DDx, but ddx bead update --claim currently hardcodes assignee ddx and forces HELIX to reimplement claim behavior in its wrapper. Implement the FEAT-004 / SD-004 design for explicit assignee-aware claim and unclaim support directly in DDx CLI and store code, preserve deterministic claimed-at and claimed-pid behavior, and keep existing beads.jsonl data readable. If the design task introduces a narrower governing artifact, update this issue's spec-id before coding. Generate or update deterministic acceptance tests and lower-level store tests rather than relying on manual verification.
title: Implement assignee-aware claim/unclaim support in ddx bead

acceptance: Metric observations are durably recorded with metric identity, timestamp, captured values, derived aggregates, and provenance; DDx exposes a documented surface to inspect ordered history and trend data for a metric; and deterministic tests cover atomic or serialized writes, retrieval/filter behavior, artifact-link integrity, and history-backed improvement tracking.
description: After the metric boundary artifacts land, implement durable DDx storage for metric observations over time. Store metric run records in a workflow-agnostic form keyed to the governing metric artifact and executable definition identity, with enough context to inspect prior runs, compare current results against history, and track improvements over time. The implementation should define an inspect/query surface for ordered history and trend reporting without teaching DDx about HELIX stage progression. Add deterministic tests for storage layout, append behavior, retrieval, trend calculations, and failure handling.
title: Implement durable metric observation history and trend inspection

acceptance: The review records planning evidence, implementation evidence, classification, and a concrete follow-up plan for any FEAT-010 drift found.
description: Review the metric-specific CLI and storage layer against FEAT-010. Confirm whether the current .ddx/metrics implementation is a divergence from the generic ddx exec model and what follow-up work is required.
title: Metric runtime store diverges from FEAT-010

acceptance: ddx exec run <definition-id> executes a definition and persists an immutable run record with raw logs, structured result, terminal status, timing, and linked artifact IDs. ddx exec history --artifact <id> returns runs for that artifact.
description: HELIX FEAT-005 depends on ddx exec being functional enough to register definitions and persist run records. This is the minimum viable execution runtime needed for HELIX integration.
title: Ship ddx exec run with execution definition registration and run capture

acceptance: cli/internal/exec/store.go and cli/internal/metric/store.go use the collection-aware bead store instead of bespoke metadata files; JSONL-backed runtime metadata lands in named collection files while large payloads remain sidecar attachments; external backends receive logical collection names without DDx assuming bd/br physical layout; compatibility or migration behavior for preexisting runtime metadata is explicit and test-covered; and go test ./internal/exec ./internal/metric ./internal/bead/... passes.
description: Background: the collection-aware bead store, bead-record schema, and storage ADR are now in place, but cli/internal/exec/store.go and cli/internal/metric/store.go still own bespoke runtime metadata layouts. Implement the storage migration described by ADR-004, FEAT-010, SD-005, and TD-005: persist execution and metric metadata through bead-backed named collections, keep large payloads as sidecar attachments referenced from bead records, preserve the default tracker collection boundary, and keep external backends collection-logical rather than file-prescriptive. Before cutting code, make the compatibility and migration behavior explicit for any existing .ddx/exec or .ddx/metrics metadata; then add deterministic store and command-path tests for create/run/read/history behavior.
title: Migrate exec and metric metadata stores onto bead-backed collections

acceptance: The DDx tracker contains dependency-gated child tasks that implement the generic `ddx exec` substrate, layer metric-specific behavior on top without reintroducing a separate metric store, and remediate migration/API/docs/test fallout. Each child references the governing execution and metric artifacts, names concrete tests, and supersedes the earlier closed metric-only implementation plan.
description: Background: DDx frame and design docs now define a generic execution substrate (`FEAT-010`, `SD-005`, `TD-005`, `TP-005`) where artifacts stay declarative and runtime evidence lives in `ddx exec` definitions and runs. The code in the current worktree has not been reconciled to that model: it still exposes a metric-specific CLI and store (`cli/cmd/metric.go`, `cli/internal/metric/*`) and still writes to `.ddx/metrics/*`. That means the documented abstraction and the implementation target have diverged. This epic replaces the earlier closed metric backlog slice as the actionable implementation contract. Scope this tranche to: (1) implementing the generic execution substrate, (2) re-layering metrics as a specialization over that substrate, and (3) remediating storage, API, migration, tests, and docs so the codebase and specs agree. Require each child task to preserve artifact-generic boundaries, generate deterministic tests, and state any compatibility behavior explicitly.
title: Reconcile metric implementation with the DDx execution model

acceptance: Planning stack findings are captured in the repo alignment report with evidence and a classification.
description: Review product vision, PRD, feature specs, architecture, ADRs, implementation plan, and artifact linkage for repo-level alignment.
title: Review planning stack and governance

acceptance: Core CLI findings are captured in the repo alignment report with evidence and a classification.
description: Review the CLI command surface and the core engines behind docs, beads, agent dispatch, and doc graph behavior.
title: Review core CLI, doc graph, bead, and agent surfaces

acceptance: A consolidated alignment report exists in docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews and all area findings are linked to tracker issues.
description: Repo-wide alignment review for DDx against the authoritative planning stack.
title: HELIX alignment review: repo

acceptance: For a selected harness, ddx agent can list the available reasoning levels and models before invocation. The output is deterministic, reflects per-harness config and built-in defaults, and returns a clear error for unknown or unavailable harnesses. Existing agent invocation behavior continues to honor flags, config, and defaults.
description: The agent wrapper currently resolves harness/model selection, but it cannot robustly enumerate the available reasoning levels and model choices for a selected harness. This makes invocation validation and UX brittle when new harnesses or model mappings are added.
title: Add agent capability introspection for reasoning levels and models

acceptance: Helix queue commands can read .ddx/beads.jsonl deterministically even when the file contains partially written, malformed, or concurrently updated records. Writer behavior is documented or enforced so emitted records remain parseable, and reader-side errors are reported with enough context to isolate the bad record without derailing the entire queue.
description: Helix queue processing hit parse/fingerprint instability while reading .ddx/beads.jsonl, exposing a gap between how entries are written and how they are consumed. We need to determine whether the writer must be stricter or the reader must tolerate malformed/partial records better, then fix the chosen path.
title: Harden beads.jsonl queue handling

acceptance: Server findings are captured in the repo alignment report with evidence and a classification.
description: Review the HTTP API and MCP transport surfaces against FEAT-002 and related design artifacts.
title: Review server and MCP surfaces

acceptance: Registry findings are captured in the repo alignment report with evidence and a classification.
description: Review package registry and install/update behavior against FEAT-009 and related integrity design.
title: Review registry and package install surfaces

acceptance: Web findings are captured in the repo alignment report with evidence and a classification.
description: Review the embedded SPA and the public website against FEAT-003 and FEAT-008.
title: Review web surfaces and public website
